title: Monster Hunter Wilds

gpu: NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1660 Ti
notes:
To get the game to stop instantly CTD'ing on launch, I had to get rid of CrashReport.exe and .dll (I put them in a new subfolder just in case). Then I was hit with the dreaded Fatal D3D error which seems to afflict people trying to run the game through almost any version of Proton on an nVidia GPU.

The fix for me was to find Proton Experimental in my game library, right-click > properties > betas > enable Bleeding Edge, and set MHW to use that. Like it says when you select this, BACK UP YOUR PREFIXES FIRST if you're using Experimental to get any other games running.

Now I can compile shaders and wait for the beta test to actually go live.
title: Monster Hunter Wilds

gpu: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4090
notes:
Still awaiting unlock of Beta.  To get working with NVidia drivers on Linux:

Find "Proton Experimental" in your library.  Go to its properties.  Click on the "Betas" tab on the side panel.  Choose "bleeding-edge - latest and untested dxvk...".  PC will download necessary proton bits.  Game will then launch, pre-render textures, allow you to choose language, then provide the error that servers are not yet online.

gpu: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3050

Reading through https://steamcommunity.com/app/3065170/discussions/0/4632608282461148515/ it seems that the game is doing this on systems with NVIDIA GPUs, and that a patch to the issue was already pushed to the bleeding edge build of proton. Hopefully in the future it will be part of the default boot when it gets upstreamed, but for now I am able to compile the shaders and get the unable to connect to server error, which is as far as I can confirm this works with the beta not being launched yet

title: Monster Hunter Wilds

gpu: 4.6 (Compatibility Profile) Mesa 24.1.7
notes:
Using bleeding-edge Proton can fix the issue of the game crashing while (or before) compiling shaders.
Right click Proton Experimental in Steam Library ---- properties ---- betas ---- bleeding-edge, 
and setting MH Wilds to use Proton Experimental.

Launch option: 

VKD3D_DISABLE_EXTENSIONS=VK_NV_low_latency2 %command%

It can fix the issue of the game crashing after the RE engine screen.

gpu: AMD Radeon RX 7900 XTX (radeonsi, navi31, LLVM 18.1.8, DRM 3.59, 6.11.5-zen1-1-zen)
notes:
Find Proton Experimental in steam game list > properties > betas > bleeding edge

Game will no longer crash on startup and will allow shader compilation. Solution from reddit /r/linuxgaming
title: Monster Hunter Wilds

gpu: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3080 Ti
notes:
1. Go into your Steam Library
2. Search for "Proton Experimental"
3. Right click "Proton Experimental" and click on "Properties"
4. Click on "Betas"
5.  Click the dropdown menu next to "Beta Participation"
6. Select "bleeding-edge - latest and untested dxvk, vkd3d-proton and wine changes; backup your prefixes before using"
7. Find "Monster Hunter Wilds beta" in your library, right click and go into "Properties"
8. Enter this in "Launch Arguments": VKD3D_DISABLE_EXTENSIONS=VK_NV_low_latency2

title: Monster Hunter Wilds

gpu: NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1660 Ti
notes:
If you get an immediate CTD on launch with no error message, take the CrashReport.exe and .dll from the game's folder and move them somewhere else.
If you then get a Fatal D3D error, this seems to be from trying to run the game in Proton with an nVidia GPU -- find Proton Experimental under Tools in your Library, then right-click > Properties > Betas > Bleeding Edge. BACK UP YOUR PREFIXES FIRST if you're using non-bleeding edge Experimental for other games. This should get you through to shader compilation.
Once that is done, you may get a C++ Runtime Library Assertion Failed error. Add the launch option listed above to fix that and get through to the language selection screen.

Still listed as borked at time of writing since the game isn't actually live for anyone to play yet, but fingers crossed this will make it playable once the servers are up.
title: Monster Hunter Wilds

gpu: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 2060
notes:
After compiling and after the RE engine logo apperas, it instantly crashes with the next error code
Wine error Line 5772: !status && "vkLatencySleepNV"
I've tried modifying the config.ini to "Capability=DirectX11" and it failsafes to "Capability=DirectX12"

Also tried on the parameters to set gfx_nvnUseLowLatency 0 sience that was the parameter i used on Apex Legends a time ago and hoped it would work too on wilds, but it doesn't

Let's hope that when it launches on 28 of feb, valve or GE drops an update fixing this
